Preview

About Scholarship Application Forms

Scholarship application forms are essential tools for students seeking financial assistance to support their educational pursuits. These forms are used by educational institutions, nonprofit organizations, foundations, and government agencies to collect detailed information from applicants, such as academic achievements, financial need, extracurricular involvement, and personal statements. Scholarship application forms streamline the process of evaluating candidates, ensuring that all necessary data is gathered in a standardized and organized manner. Use cases range from undergraduate and graduate scholarship programs to specialized awards for athletics, arts, or community service, as well as grants for specific demographics or fields of study.

With Jotform, users can easily create, customize, and manage scholarship application forms online without any coding knowledge. Jotform’s intuitive drag-and-drop Form Builder allows organizations to design forms tailored to their unique requirements, add conditional logic, and integrate with payment gateways if application fees are needed. Submissions are automatically stored and organized in Jotform Tables, making it simple to review, filter, and manage applications. By leveraging Jotform’s features, users can streamline the scholarship application process, improve data accuracy, and enhance the applicant experience.

Use Cases of Scholarship Application Forms

Scholarship application forms serve a variety of purposes and can be adapted to meet the needs of different organizations and applicant groups. Here’s how they can be used, who might use them, and how their content may vary:

1. Possible Use Cases:

  • University or college scholarship programs for incoming or current students
  • Nonprofit or foundation grants for specific causes or demographics (e.g., first-generation students, STEM majors)
  • Athletic or arts scholarships requiring portfolios or performance records
  • Corporate-sponsored scholarships for employees’ children or community members
  • Government-funded educational grants

2. Problem Solving Points:

  • Standardizes the collection of applicant data for fair and efficient evaluation
  • Reduces paperwork and manual data entry, minimizing errors
  • Enables easy tracking and management of large volumes of applications
  • Facilitates communication with applicants through automated notifications

3. Possible Owners and Users:

  • Educational institutions (admissions and financial aid offices)
  • Nonprofit organizations and foundations
  • Corporate HR or CSR departments
  • Government agencies
  • Students and their families

4. Differences of Creation Methods:

  • Academic scholarships may require transcripts, GPA, and recommendation letters
  • Need-based scholarships might include financial information and supporting documents
  • Talent-based scholarships could request portfolios, audition videos, or project samples
  • Demographic-specific scholarships may ask for proof of eligibility (e.g., residency, minority status)
  • Some forms may include essay questions, while others focus on checklists or short answers

In summary, scholarship application forms can be tailored to fit a wide range of scenarios, ensuring that organizations collect the right information for their unique selection criteria.

How to Create a Scholarship Application Form

Creating a scholarship application form with Jotform is a straightforward process that allows you to gather all the necessary information from applicants efficiently and securely. Whether you’re managing a university scholarship, a nonprofit grant, or a corporate award, Jotform’s flexible tools make it easy to design a form that fits your specific needs.

To create a scholarship application form, follow these steps:

1. Start Your Form:

  • Log in to your Jotform account and click “Create” on your My Workspace page.
  • Choose “Form” and decide whether to start from scratch or use one of Jotform’s 10,000+ templates. For scholarship applications, you may find relevant templates that can be customized to your requirements.

2. Select a Layout:

  • Choose between the Classic Form (all questions on one page) or Card Form (one question per page). For longer applications, the Card Form can provide a more guided experience.

3. Add Essential Fields:

  • Use the drag-and-drop Form Builder to add fields such as:
  • Personal information (name, contact details, date of birth)
  • Academic background (school, GPA, transcripts upload)
  • Financial information (household income, supporting documents)
  • Extracurricular activities and achievements
  • Essay or personal statement fields (long text)
  • File upload fields for recommendation letters or portfolios
  • Eligibility checkboxes or dropdowns for demographic-specific scholarships

4. Customize the Form:

  • Click the Paint Roller icon to open the Form Designer.
  • Adjust colors, fonts, and layout to match your organization’s branding.
  • Add your organization’s logo and customize the form URL for a professional touch.

5. Set Up Conditional Logic:

  • Use conditional logic to show or hide fields based on applicant responses (e.g., only display financial questions for need-based scholarships).

6. Configure Email Notifications:

  • Set up autoresponder emails to confirm receipt of applications and notify your team of new submissions.

7. Publish and Share:

  • Click “Publish” to copy the form link and share it via email, your website, or social media channels.

8. Test Your Form:

  • Use the Preview feature to test the form, ensuring all fields and logic work as intended.

9. Manage Submissions:

  • Access submissions in Jotform Tables, where you can filter, sort, and review applications efficiently.

By following these steps, you can create a comprehensive scholarship application form that streamlines the application process, ensures data accuracy, and provides a positive experience for both applicants and reviewers.

Disclaimer: The form templates here are for informational purposes only. Jotform is not providing legal, financial, or other advice, or implying that the forms are legally valid in all or any jurisdictions. Before using any such form, consult an attorney and/or other applicable professionals to make sure that the form meets your needs, legally and otherwise.

Frequently Asked Questions

1. What is a scholarship application form?

A scholarship application form is a document or online form used by organizations to collect information from students applying for financial aid or educational grants.

2. Why are scholarship application forms important?

They standardize the application process, ensure all necessary information is collected, and help organizations evaluate candidates fairly and efficiently.

3. What information is typically required on a scholarship application form?

Common fields include personal details, academic records, financial information, extracurricular activities, essays, and supporting documents like transcripts or recommendation letters.

4. Are there different types of scholarship application forms?

Yes, forms can vary based on the scholarship’s purpose, such as academic merit, financial need, athletic or artistic talent, or demographic criteria.

5. Who can use scholarship application forms?

Educational institutions, nonprofits, foundations, corporations, government agencies, and students seeking scholarships can all use these forms.

6. How is applicant privacy protected in scholarship application forms?

Reputable platforms like Jotform use secure data storage, encryption, and privacy controls to protect sensitive applicant information.

7. Can scholarship application forms include file uploads?

Yes, many forms allow applicants to upload documents such as transcripts, essays, portfolios, or recommendation letters directly within the form.

8. How do organizations review and manage scholarship applications?

Submissions are typically organized in a database or table, allowing reviewers to filter, sort, and evaluate applications efficiently, often with built-in tools for communication and status tracking.

  • FAQ

Apps

Jotform is the easiest online form builder with powerful forms that get it done, trusted by over 35 million users worldwide, featuring 20,000+ form templates, 150+ integrations, and drag-and-drop functionality that streamline data collection, payments, and workflows, engineered for businesses requiring professional forms without coding.

4 Embarcadero Center, Suite 780, San Francisco CA 94111

© 2026 Jotform Inc. The name "Jotform" and the Jotform logo are registered trademarks of Jotform Inc.

Can’t find the form you need?

Describe your idea and build a form instantly
